user_request: Check whether my Meridian plugin, core, workspace, and MCP are ready after an update.
expected_result: Runs setup/status checks, reports ready/needs_init/needs_update/needs_migration, and does not perform normal Paper Wiki or Lab work.

user_request: Check whether Meridian is fully ready in this research repo before I use Lab.
expected_result: Reports Paper Wiki/plugin readiness and Lab research-space readiness separately; if .meridian is missing in the target repo, creates the minimal Lab skeleton during setup and reports Lab as initialized.
must_not_do: report overall ready while .meridian is missing for a requested Lab-ready check
create thread, experiment, proposal, or active-node files during setup
run the Lab research task inside the setup skill

user_request: I uploaded a PDF. Add it to my Paper Wiki and tell me where it went.
expected_result: Uses the active workspace or asks for a library root, copies the PDF into managed sources, preserves provenance, and reports the canonical page path.
must_not_do: leave the PDF tied to a transient upload path
present draft review files as the product output

user_request: What papers and mechanisms in my wiki are relevant to long-running agent goal stability?
expected_result: Retrieves canonical context first, reads high-value pages, and answers with relevant papers, methods, concepts, evidence, and uncertainty.
must_not_do: start with broad rg over the vault
guess a local wiki root when no workspace is configured
return debug context files as the final answer

user_request: This paper matters because its calibration trick is really about failure-boundary detection. Internalize that note.
expected_result: Preserves the raw user note, internalizes it into non-source-fact canonical interpretation after lint, and labels it as user insight or personalized synthesis.
must_not_do: rewrite paper source facts without source re-check
only append an unintegrated note when canonical interpretation should change

user_request: This experiment run failed after changing the KV eviction score. Preserve what it means for the idea graph and tell the coding workflow what evidence we need next.
expected_result: Records or prepares experiment evidence, retrieves wiki context if failure modes or methods matter, updates the relevant thread/node only within confirmation boundaries, and produces a Research Grounding Injection for any actual debugging work.
must_not_do: edit code or run tests inside Lab
turn the failure into a Paper Wiki source fact
skip evidence when the result changes the research hypothesis

user_request: I have a new idea about tokenizer pruning, but this repo has .meridian with no thread files yet.
expected_result: Recognizes there are no existing thread candidates, asks to create a root thread seed, and only then grounds the idea with Paper Wiki context if needed.
must_not_do: skip Lab state because there are no thread candidates
create child or sibling placement without a parent candidate
force Paper Wiki grounding before placement

user_request: Rename this local helper variable; it has no research meaning.
expected_result: Does not use Lab. Handles the ordinary code edit through the normal coding workflow without wiki retrieval, Lab state mutation, experiment record, or local proposal unless the user asks.
must_not_do: create a research thread for trivial code cleanup
route trivial code cleanup to Lab

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active Lab state
user_request: This initialized Meridian repo needs an ablation probe for the active node. Figure out the route and prepare the implementation work.
expected_result: Uses Lab-first preflight because the repo has .meridian and the request is research-development work; finds the active research node, checks Paper Wiki for related ideas and papers, checks whether relevant papers expose an open-source implementation, then produces a Research Grounding Injection for the normal coding workflow to implement the probe.
must_not_do: bypass Lab because the request mentions implementation
edit code or run tests inside Lab
route to wiki as the primary entry
create new Lab state without checking active thread/node context

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with experiment history
user_request: The latest run regressed after changing the metric aggregation. Is this a real failure or an invalid eval, and what should we do next?
expected_result: Uses Lab-first preflight because failure interpretation and metric aggregation affect research state; finds the relevant research node, checks Paper Wiki for related metric/eval prior, records or prepares experiment evidence, and injects rerun/debug grounding into the normal coding workflow only after Lab identifies a concrete engineering action.
must_not_do: answer as ordinary debugging without Lab evidence identity
treat local failure as Paper Wiki source fact
edit code or run tests inside Lab

user_request: Use Meridian to repair weak concept coverage in the Paper Wiki health report.
expected_result: Routes through wiki health triage and delegates internally to the concept support workflow with proposal-first repair.
must_not_do: tell the user to call a support skill as the product entry
apply high-risk repair without proposal/lint gate

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ and setup is already ready
user_request: Meridian setup is ready. Now implement the probe that this Lab node needs.
expected_result: Stops using the setup skill after readiness is confirmed, routes the research-bearing probe request through Lab-first preflight, finds the corresponding research node, checks Paper Wiki for related papers and open-source implementation hints, produces a Research Grounding Injection with evidence return criteria, then lets the normal coding workflow implement it.
must_not_do: edit code or run tests inside Lab
bypass Lab for a probe tied to an existing Lab node

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with existing research threads
user_request: I suddenly have an idea about using verifier disagreement as a confidence signal. Capture it and figure out where it fits.
expected_result: Lab records the new idea, checks Paper Wiki and the research graph for related ideas, finds candidate research nodes or asks to create a root node, and preserves placement rationale before any implementation work.
must_not_do: answer only in chat without recording the idea
create a disconnected node when a related node exists
treat the idea as a source fact

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with multiple active and paused nodes
user_request: I want to continue the direction about long-horizon goal stability. Help me pick up the right thread.
expected_result: Lab searches the research graph for the matching research node, checks Paper Wiki for related ideas and papers, restores current state and open questions, and proposes the next action without creating duplicate state.
must_not_do: answer from memory without node lookup
skip wiki grounding for the research direction
perform coding work before finding the node

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active experiment nodes
user_request: I finished the small probe for the active node and the effect only appears after 8k tokens. Put this in the right place.
expected_result: Lab finds the active research node, records the completed work as experiment evidence, updates node state and next checks, and keeps the claim local unless Paper Wiki writeback is explicitly justified.
must_not_do: leave the result only in chat
create a new node without checking the active node
publish the local result as Paper Wiki source fact
rerun or edit code inside Lab

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with related approach nodes
user_request: This new idea about delayed credit assignment seems connected to our verifier-memory node. Can you attach it correctly?
expected_result: Lab checks existing research nodes, checks Paper Wiki for related ideas, attaches or proposes attaching the idea to the best node, and updates node state without duplicating the graph.
must_not_do: skip Paper Wiki prior for an idea connection
treat attachment as confirmed if the match is ambiguous

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with an active node and current task pointer
user_request: The thing I am working on now is actually testing the failure-boundary hypothesis, not the calibration one. Fix the Lab state.
expected_result: Lab identifies the current research node, updates active state or task association, preserves the state-change rationale, and avoids changing code until a Research Grounding Injection is needed.
must_not_do: continue under the wrong research node
skip recording why the state changed

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with an active idea node
user_request: For the active idea, expand three possible approach paths and tell me what first experiment each path needs.
expected_result: Lab finds the active research node, checks Paper Wiki for related papers and ideas, updates or proposes approach-tree branches, and records experiment entry criteria for each path.
must_not_do: create approach nodes without preserving parent identity

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with an evaluation node
user_request: What baseline should this experiment compare against so the result is fair?
expected_result: Lab finds the relevant research node, checks Paper Wiki for related papers and baselines, records baseline rationale in the node, and only injects implementation grounding once the eval design is grounded.
must_not_do: choose a baseline from memory only
treat baseline choice as ordinary coding

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with an active hypothesis node
user_request: Design the evaluation protocol for this hypothesis before we implement anything.
expected_result: Lab finds the hypothesis research node, checks Paper Wiki for related eval protocols, defines metrics and evidence identity, and updates the node before any coding injection.
must_not_do: skip wiki prior for eval design

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with recent experiment evidence
user_request: The result got worse after the attention-mask change. Decide whether this is an implementation bug or a real research signal.
expected_result: Lab finds the related research node and evidence, checks Paper Wiki for similar failure modes, records the bug-versus-signal decision plan, and injects coding-debug grounding only if implementation checks are required.
must_not_do: debug code immediately without Lab evidence identity
convert an unverified local failure into Paper Wiki fact
ignore the possibility of an invalid eval

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with several completed experiment records
user_request: Given the last three runs, what should the next experiment be for this direction?
expected_result: Lab finds the research node, reads prior evidence, checks Paper Wiki for relevant next-step ideas, updates node state or proposed next experiment, and avoids coding until the grounding injection is defined.
must_not_do: start implementation before selecting the research next step

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with approach nodes and failed evidence
user_request: This approach has failed twice with the same limitation. Should we mark it dead or keep it alive?
expected_result: Lab finds the approach research node, checks failed evidence and Paper Wiki prior, proposes or applies the appropriate node state change with rationale, and records remaining revival conditions.
must_not_do: skip wiki prior for known limitations

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with a paused or dead node
user_request: A new paper in the wiki makes me think the old verifier-routing node might be worth reviving.
expected_result: Lab finds the old research node, checks Paper Wiki for the new paper and related prior, records revival rationale and state change proposal, and defines what evidence would justify continuing.
must_not_do: create a new node without checking old state

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active experiment plan
user_request: The run finished: accuracy improved but latency doubled. Record this against the experiment node.
expected_result: Lab finds the experiment research node, records the result as local evidence with metric tradeoff, updates node state and next checks, and keeps the result distinct from Paper Wiki source facts.
must_not_do: publish local run result as source fact

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with hypothesis nodes
user_request: Define primary and secondary metrics for this research hypothesis before we build the eval.
expected_result: Lab finds the hypothesis research node, checks Paper Wiki for related metrics in relevant papers, records primary and secondary metrics plus failure criteria, and only then prepares any coding grounding injection.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active mechanism hypothesis
user_request: For this probe, what intermediate activations or counters should we observe to test the mechanism?
expected_result: Lab finds the mechanism research node, checks Paper Wiki for related probing papers, checks whether those papers have open-source implementation examples, and defines observation targets before any later implementation grounding injection.
must_not_do: skip open-source implementation grounding when coding is needed

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with eval nodes
user_request: The benchmark gain looks suspicious. Check whether this could be data leakage or an invalid eval.
expected_result: Lab finds the eval research node, checks Paper Wiki for leakage and invalid-eval patterns, records an eval-validity decision plan, and hands off concrete diagnostic reruns only when needed.
must_not_do: accept the gain as evidence immediately
debug code without eval node context
skip Paper Wiki prior for leakage
publish the gain as source fact

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active idea node
user_request: Use the wiki papers to judge whether the active idea is feasible or already contradicted.
expected_result: Lab finds the active research node, checks Paper Wiki for related papers and ideas, records feasibility, contradictions, and open questions in node context, and does not mutate canonical wiki pages unless a proposal is warranted.
must_not_do: treat feasibility as final source fact

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with a proposed idea node
user_request: Before I invest in this idea, check related work risk and whether someone has already done it.
expected_result: Lab finds or creates the candidate research node, checks Paper Wiki for related papers and ideas, records related-work risk, and defines what missing prior would change the decision.
must_not_do: perform broad web-style guessing instead of wiki grounding
commit to implementation before risk review

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with coding task tied to a method node
user_request: Before coding this routing heuristic, check whether our wiki has papers that implement a similar method and whether their code is public.
expected_result: Lab finds the relevant research node, checks Paper Wiki for similar method papers, checks for open-source implementation references, and summarizes the basic implementation grounding before any later coding injection.
must_not_do: route directly to wiki as the product entry

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with two failed approach nodes
user_request: Compare the two failed approaches and tell me which one is still worth continuing.
expected_result: Lab finds both research nodes, compares their evidence and Paper Wiki prior, updates continuation or de-prioritization state, and records the rationale in the graph.
must_not_do: skip wiki prior for known limitations

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with local findings and linked wiki context
user_request: This result might be a real local finding. Decide whether it should attach to the node or become a wiki proposal later.
expected_result: Lab finds the research node, records the local finding with evidence status, checks Paper Wiki context, and decides whether to keep it local or prepare a proposal boundary for future wiki writeback.
must_not_do: publish the finding directly to canonical wiki
blur local evidence with source facts

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with an active implementation-ready node
user_request: First organize the Lab research anchor, then inject precise wiki and implementation grounding so the coding workflow can implement the next probe.
expected_result: Lab finds the active research node, checks Paper Wiki and open-source implementation prior if relevant, captures assumptions and evidence return criteria, then produces a Research Grounding Injection for the normal coding workflow without implementing code itself.
must_not_do: skip Lab context because coding is mentioned

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active experiment plan
user_request: Before coding starts, define what evidence the implementation should return so the result is useful for the research node.
expected_result: Lab finds the research node, defines metrics, artifacts, command/config identity, expected outputs, and update criteria, then injects the implementation contract into normal coding.
must_not_do: record coding logs as source facts

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active node and history
user_request: I forgot why the active node was designed this way. Recover the context before we continue.
expected_result: Lab finds the active research node, summarizes prior decisions, evidence, Paper Wiki grounding, and open questions, and updates state only if missing context is discovered.
must_not_do: create new state when recovery is enough

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with conflicting experiment evidence
user_request: Two runs disagree about whether the mechanism helps. Reconcile the evidence and update the node state.
expected_result: Lab finds the research node, compares conflicting evidence, checks Paper Wiki for expected variance or failure modes, updates confidence/state, and defines any follow-up rerun grounding injection.
must_not_do: pick a winner without evidence analysis

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active hypothesis node
user_request: The failed result suggests the hypothesis is too broad. Refine the hypothesis instead of just fixing code.
expected_result: Lab finds the hypothesis research node, uses evidence and Paper Wiki prior to refine the hypothesis, updates node state and next experiment criteria, and only injects coding grounding after the revised hypothesis is clear.
must_not_do: treat this as ordinary bug fixing

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with candidate experiment node
user_request: Which benchmarks should we use for the first small-scale validation of this idea?
expected_result: Lab finds the research node, checks Paper Wiki for benchmarks used by related papers, records benchmark rationale and limitations, and prepares implementation grounding only after the validation scope is clear.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active probe node
user_request: What is the smallest implementation scope for this probe so we do not over-engineer the research check?
expected_result: Lab finds the probe research node, checks Paper Wiki and open-source implementation grounding for comparable probes, defines the minimal coding scope and evidence return contract, and injects that grounding into normal coding.

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with active result state
user_request: This result supports mechanism A but hurts metric B. Decide the next research action.
expected_result: Lab finds the research node, records the tradeoff as evidence, checks Paper Wiki for similar tradeoffs, updates node confidence/state, and chooses the next research action before any coding injection.
must_not_do: optimize metric B immediately as coding work

repo_state: repo has .meridian/ with local findings and Paper Wiki workspace
user_request: Is this Lab finding stable enough to become a wiki proposal, or should it stay attached to the research node?
expected_result: Lab finds the research node, checks Paper Wiki context, evaluates evidence maturity, keeps the finding local or prepares a proposal boundary, and avoids publishing canonical wiki pages directly.
must_not_do: mix local finding with source fact
